title: Functional abdominal pain documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.
examination.rationale: The exact red-flag recommendation supports clinician assessment and examination for concerning context without populating negatives.
examination.evidence_extract: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.
investigations.rationale: The exact adult IBS section supports chronic abdominal pain, relation to defaecation, bowel pattern, bloating, food association, urgency, incomplete evacuation, mucus, and impact without diagnosing IBS or functional pain.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.
investigations.rationale: The exact diagnostic-test section supports recording already reviewed blood or coeliac testing in appropriately assessed adults without ordering tests.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.
investigations.rationale: The exact follow-up section supports clinician-entered review, follow-up, and safety net without generating treatment.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

source_limitations: Pain phenotype, duration criteria, bowel association, red flags, diagnosis, tests, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
